task: I’m trying to post a used item online and want to avoid making the listing look sloppy. Can you help me tighten up the title, photos, and message I should send if someone asks for a pickup time?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a clean, simple way to make it look polished.

## 1) Title
Use:
**[Brand] [Item name] – [condition] – [key detail]**

Examples:
- **IKEA Poäng Chair – Good condition – Pickup only**
- **Apple AirPods Pro (2nd Gen) – Like new – Includes case**
- **Men’s North Face Jacket – Size M – Excellent condition**

Tips:
- Keep it short
- Put the most important details first
- Avoid ALL CAPS, emojis, and extra words like “must see!!!”

## 2) Photos
To make the listing look neat:
- Use natural light
- Take photos against a plain background
- Include:
  - front
  - back
  - sides
  - close-ups of any wear/damage
  - labels/serial numbers if relevant
- Crop out clutter
- Make sure the item is centered and in focus

If there’s damage, show it clearly — that builds trust.

## 3) Message for pickup time
Here’s a polished message you can send:

**“Hi, thanks for your interest. I’m available for pickup on [day/time options]. Let me know what works best for you.”**

If you want it a little warmer:
**“Hi! Thanks for reaching out. I’m available for pickup [day/time options]. Feel free to send a time that works for you.”**

If you want, I can also help you:
- rewrite your exact listing title
- draft the full item description
- make your pickup message sound more friendly or more professional

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, this is fine, but I need it a little more specific. Title part’s okay. The pickup message is too clean, like a school email. I’d want something shorter, normal, not weirdly formal.

Also if the item has scratches or a missing piece, I need wording for that without making it sound trashed. And maybe 2-3 title versions, not just one formula.

turns.agent_response:
Yep — here’s a more natural version.

## Pickup message
Short, normal options:

- **“Hey, I’m free [day/time]. Let me know what works.”**
- **“Hi — I can do pickup [day/time].”**
- **“I’m available [day/time]. If that works, just message me.”**
- **“Sure, I can do [day/time].”**

If you want it a little friendlier:
- **“Hey! I’m around [day/time] if that works for you.”**

## Wording for scratches / missing piece
You want to be honest without sounding dramatic. Use wording like:

### Scratches / wear
- **“Has some light scratches from normal use.”**
- **“Shows minor cosmetic wear, but works as expected.”**
- **“A few small marks, nothing major.”**
- **“Used condition with visible wear.”**

### Missing piece
- **“Missing [part], but the rest is in good shape.”**
- **“One piece is missing; priced accordingly.”**
- **“Does not include [part].”**
- **“Missing [part], otherwise fully functional.”**

### If you want it to sound softer
- **“Not perfect, but still in solid condition.”**
- **“Has some cosmetic wear, but still looks good overall.”**
- **“Used, with a few signs of wear from normal use.”**

## 2–3 title versions
Here are a few styles you can use:

### 1. Straightforward
**[Brand] [Item Name] – Used – Pickup Only**

### 2. Slightly cleaner / more detailed
**[Brand] [Item Name] – Good Condition – Minor Wear**

### 3. If there’s a flaw
**[Brand] [Item Name] – Good Condition – Small Scratches / Missing [Part]**

If you want, send me the actual item details and I’ll write:
- 3 title options
- a short pickup reply
- a clean description with the flaw wording built in

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, closer. Need it a little less stiff though.

Pickup message should sound more like:
- "Hey, I’m free [day/time]."
- "I can do [day/time] if that works."
- "Yeah, [day/time] is good for me."

Stuff like that. Short. Not "Hi —" every time.

For scratches/missing piece, I’d want wording that sounds normal, not like a warning label:
- "Has some scratches from use, but still looks good."
- "Missing [part], but everything else is there."
- "Used condition, some wear, no big issues."
- "A couple marks, nothing that really stands out."

And yeah, give me 2–3 title versions that feel more like actual marketplace titles, not a template. Like one plain, one cleaner, one a little more salesy.

turns.agent_response:
Got it — more natural, less stiff.

## Pickup message options
- **“Hey, I’m free [day/time].”**
- **“I can do [day/time] if that works.”**
- **“Yeah, [day/time] is good for me.”**
- **“I’m around [day/time].”**
- **“[Day/time] works for me.”**

If you want a tiny bit warmer:
- **“Hey, I’m free [day/time] if you want to grab it then.”**
- **“I can do [day/time] — just let me know.”**

## Wording for scratches / missing piece
More normal, marketplace-style phrasing:

### Scratches / wear
- **“Has some scratches from use, but still looks good.”**
- **“Used condition with a few marks.”**
- **“A couple cosmetic scratches, nothing major.”**
- **“Some wear from normal use, but overall in good shape.”**

### Missing piece
- **“Missing [part], but everything else is there.”**
- **“Doesn’t include [part], otherwise in good shape.”**
- **“Missing [part], but still works fine.”**
- **“One piece is missing, but the rest is complete.”**

### Softer / more casual
- **“Used condition, some wear, no big issues.”**
- **“A few marks here and there, but still looks nice.”**
- **“Not perfect, but still in solid shape.”**

## 2–3 title versions
Here are three styles:

### 1. Plain
**[Brand] [Item Name] – Used**

### 2. Cleaner
**[Brand] [Item Name] – Good Condition**

### 3. A little more salesy
**[Brand] [Item Name] – Good Shape – Minor Wear**

If there’s a flaw, you can keep it natural:
- **[Brand] [Item Name] – Good Condition – Minor Scratches**
- **[Brand] [Item Name] – Missing [Part] – Otherwise Good**

If you want, send me the actual item and I’ll turn it into:
- 3 real title options
- a short pickup reply
- a natural-sounding condition line

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that’s basically it.

Pickup message:
- “Hey, I’m free [day/time].”
- “I can do [day/time] if that works.”
- “Yeah, [day/time] is good for me.”
- “I’m around [day/time].”
- “[Day/time] works for me.”

For scratches / missing piece:
- “Has some scratches from use, but still looks good.”
- “Used condition with a few marks.”
- “A couple cosmetic scratches, nothing major.”
- “Some wear from normal use, but overall in good shape.”
- “Missing [part], but everything else is there.”
- “Doesn’t include [part], otherwise in good shape.”
- “Missing [part], but still works fine.”
- “One piece is missing, but the rest is complete.”

Titles:
- “[Brand] [Item Name] – Used”
- “[Brand] [Item Name] – Good Condition”
- “[Brand] [Item Name] – Good Shape – Minor Wear”

That’s the right lane. Simple, normal, not trying too hard.
